I spoke to attorney Marcel Ruzan on August 2, 2024 regarding a problem we were having with work done on our home. Our licensed general contractor allowed a subcontractor he didn't know very well to grind and seal our concrete floors. The sub claimed to have experience with grinding/sealing concrete but the results made it clear that he did not (see attached pictures). Our GC claimed we were just being unreasonable and began the process of putting a lien on our home for the remaining amount due after we refused to pay it.

That was when I reached out to Stone Canyon Law for advice. Marcel informed me that the laws in Arizona were not on my side, and I was obligated to send the final payment, regardless of the quality of the work. He told me I could then pay him $1K to write a demand letter and open an ROC complaint. And those were my only options.

That didn't sound right to me. My husband and I are both educated, intelligent people so we did our own research. And we discovered a statute of Arizona's Prompt Payment Act (A.R.S. 32-1181) that allows us to withhold payment under certain terms. And our circumstances met the criteria for withholding our final payment. I reached out to Marcel, sharing the new information I found and asking for confirmation of my understanding, but he never responded to me.

I found another attorney, who was clearly much more experienced representing home owners in cases such as these. She was amazing and confirmed all of my understanding of the statutes. We were able to lawfully withhold the payment until the GC confirmed the floors had not been done correctly. He agreed to refund us the money it would cost us to have them done again.

Most of the attorneys I contacted during this experience seem to only represent contractors interests, not those of the home owners. Nancy Giles of Giles Law is the attorney we ultimately worked with and she was wonderful.

reviews.text: Really awful experience lately. They are refusing to respond to my text messages or emails! I'll be updating more soon. This firm has changed its name, email address and several times change its phone number when it gets negative reviews and they go against Google policy for reviews by bribing people with gift cards very often as a way to keep the reviews up! I tithed over $5,000 to this man's church and gave him a $300 gift one time as a Christmas gift because the guy was so unresponsive. I was wondering what the hell was going on and he claimed it was money problems and whatever else. I'm really disappointed and upset that they can't even respond to me a simple text message for a question that they're obliged to answer. They delayed several things with my claims for multiple years and told me they're not at my beck and call when I was trying to follow up about some claims issues. I'm about ready to go there in person and file a complaint with the office of general counsel for the Department of Veterans Affairs!! You're telling me to contact the office but I've been texting and emailing for weeks and no response! Your holding up my claims and benefits now and I will report to the State Bar if need be. Attorney also muttered for me to shut up during a recorded VA hearing with a Veteran's Law Judge. I tried to overlook a lot of things as an act of good faith. Getting no responses in several weeks times is acting in bad faith and delaying my claims for years which I have proof of is possible malpractice even. I didn't on a lot of this before in order to be polite. Yet my patience is now exhausted with this matter.
